description: Request for retroactive SLA exception on INC-2025-405 triage time (43 minutes vs 30-minute SLA). Alert fired during active response to concurrent SEV-1 ransomware incident INC-2025-404.
justification: INC-2025-405 (SEV-2 unauthorized IAM role assumption) was detected at 03:55 UTC on November 12 while the entire security team was actively responding to INC-2025-404 (SEV-1 ransomware, detected 02:33 UTC). The on-call security engineer (David Okafor) was leading containment of the ransomware incident and could not immediately triage the new SEV-2 alert. The SRE on-call (Mei Lin) acknowledged the PagerDuty alert at 04:05 UTC and performed initial assessment but required security engineer review for triage decision. David Okafor completed INC-2025-404 containment at 03:20 UTC and transitioned to INC-2025-405 triage at 04:38 UTC. Total delay: 43 minutes (13 minutes past SLA). The IAM role assumption was contained at 06:00 UTC — well within the 4-hour containment SLA.
compensating_controls: 1. SRE on-call acknowledged PagerDuty alert within 10 minutes and performed initial assessment. 2. The suspect IAM role's permissions were limited to read-only S3 access — blast radius was constrained. 3. CloudTrail monitoring confirmed no data exfiltration during the 43-minute triage window. 4. INC-2025-404 (SEV-1) was the higher-priority concurrent incident requiring immediate team focus.
review_notes: Valid exception. Concurrent SEV-1 incident justified the delay. Team appropriately prioritized SEV-1 ransomware containment over SEV-2 triage. SRE provided bridging coverage. Post-incident review recommended adding a second security on-call for overnight coverage — tracked as PM-2025-404 action item.

description: Request for 5-day extension on post-mortem deadline for INC-2025-389 due to forensic analysis complexity.
justification: Root cause analysis requires external forensic firm engagement. Initial 5-Why analysis inconclusive — additional disk imaging and malware analysis needed.
compensating_controls: 1. Preliminary findings shared with leadership. 2. Containment and remediation actions complete. 3. Extension limited to 5 additional business days.
review_notes: Post-mortem delivered September 29. Extension was justified — external forensics confirmed advanced persistent threat.
